title: Choose an ODE solver for stiffness and accuracy
description: Match the integrator to the system's stiffness and accuracy needs, preferring implicit methods for stiff dynamics.
body: Use when integrating a system of differential equations. Do not default to explicit Euler for a stiff system, since it forces impractically tiny steps to stay stable. Actions: assess stiffness from the spread of time constants, pick an explicit method like RK4 for non-stiff problems, and switch to an implicit solver when stiffness dominates. Validation signals include a stable solution at a practical step size and error matching the method's order in a convergence study. Risk boundary: a wrong solver choice either diverges or crawls, so escalate to an adaptive or implicit method when an explicit solver cannot stay stable at a usable step.
